text: I told my laptop I needed a break, and now it won’t stop sending me vacation ads.
My coffee is strong enough to wake up my ancestors.
I tried to be normal once. Worst two minutes of my life.
I’m not lazy, I’m on energy-saving mode.
My brain has too many tabs open, and I don’t know where the music is coming from.
I put my phone on airplane mode, but it still didn’t fly away.
I asked my dog what 2 + 2 is. He said nothing, but I trust him.
I’m not arguing, I’m just explaining why I’m right.
I cleaned my room once. It was very confusing, so I stopped.
My wallet is like an onion—opening it makes me cry.
I run like the winded.
I’m on a seafood diet. I see food and I eat it.
My phone battery lasts longer in my dreams.
I would agree with you, but then we’d both be wrong.
I started a band called 999 Megabytes. We still haven’t gotten a gig.
I’m not late, I’m just on my own time zone.
I tried to be productive today, but my couch had other plans.
I have a degree in procrastination, but I haven’t framed it yet.
I speak fluent sarcasm with a slight accent of tiredness.
I told my alarm clock we need space. It still shows up every morning.
